Ward 8 · Washington DC

A community in crisis.

Seventy-five thousand people. One grocery store. A fifteen-year life expectancy gap. This is the reality Dr. Roach set out to change.

The Crisis

This is not food insecurity.
This is food apartheid.

Ward 8 has one full-service grocery store for 75,000 residents — no regular farmers' markets. Residents survive on processed corner-store goods, driving disproportionate rates of diabetes, obesity, and hypertension. Dr. Mary A. Roach treats this as the public health emergency it is.

♛
Combating this crisis since 2005 Over 20 years of radical accessibility, clinical nutrition, and community care — and we're just getting started.
⚠ The Crisis
1
Full-service grocery store for 75,000 Ward 8 residents
15yr
Life expectancy gap vs. wealthier DC neighborhoods
our response
♛ The JJ Center
$0
Always free — no cost to every resident, every week, no exceptions
Weekly
Free farmers' market-style distributions at Oxon Run Park — corner of South Capitol St SE & 1st St SE, Washington DC
Our Response

Food is medicine.

What if the prescription was fresh produce? What if the clinic was a park? What if every family could access care — with free, no cost, no barriers?

Our Solution

A Clinically Directed Pantry

We partner with grocery stores across the DMV to bring fresh produce, lean proteins, and nutrient-dense foods directly to Ward 8 every single week.

🥦
Radical Accessibility
Outdoor, farmers market-style distributions — fresh, healthy food completely free for every family
🩺
Clinical Oversight
Food curated by MDs, PharmDs & Nutritionists to combat chronic disease
🏫
Wellness Workshops
On-site medical nutrition & chronic disease education on-site every week
🚚
DMV Grocery Network
Fresh food sourced from grocery partners across DC, Maryland & Virginia
Every Week · Rain or Shine

See it in action.

From the back of a truck to the hands of a family — this is what radical accessibility looks like.

In the Community

See Us in Action

Every week at the park
Farmers' market style — outdoors
Serving since 2005
Delivered fresh every week
No one turned away
The Vision Behind the Mission

Led by a Doctor of Pharmacy.

One pharmacist decided that Ward 8 deserved the same quality of care as any neighborhood in America. So she built it herself.

🏆 2025 Global Women Award — George Washington University & Global Woman P.E.A.C.E. Foundation
CEO & Founder

Dr. Mary A. Roach

PharmD · CEO & Founder, The JJ Center, Inc.

Our Mission

"Our mission at Jehovah Jireh Community Development Center (The JJ Center) is to promote unity in the community through quality programs that bring about abundant life for individuals of all ages, races, sexes, ethnicities, creeds, national origins, disabilities, veteran status, and gender identities in the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. We are dedicated to fostering well-being through our community-focused health clinics and other supportive programs, ensuring access to resources and nurturing environments for learning and growth."

Dr. Roach founded The JJ Center on a conviction that food insecurity is not a resource shortage — it is a public health crisis requiring a clinical response. A licensed pharmacist with a Doctor of Pharmacy degree, she has dedicated her career to the health, dignity, and economic empowerment of Ward 8 residents.

Doctor of Pharmacy (PharmD) — plus bachelor's & master's in pharmacy administration
Secured $1M+ in scholarships for high school students in the community
Licensed DC real estate agent — fighting for affordable housing in Ward 8
2025 Global Women Award — George Washington University & Global Woman P.E.A.C.E. Foundation
Building the Cornerstone Care Coalition (CCC) — community transformation for Wards 7 & 8
📞 1-877-JJ-CARHE (1-877-552-2743) ✉ hello@jjcenter.org
In the News & Recognized

The JJ Center in the Spotlight

Recognized by national organizations, universities, and media for 20+ years of impact in Ward 8.

📰 Article
"Free Food Program is Eradicating Health Disparities"
National Recreation & Parks Association (NRPA)
NRPA spotlights The JJ Center's groundbreaking approach — using a free, weekly farmers' market-style pantry in Ward 8 to combat food apartheid and health inequity head-on.
Read the article
🏆 Award
2021 Charter Day Award — Distinguished Postgraduate Achievement
Howard University · Washington, DC
Dr. Roach was named among Howard University's 2021 Charter Day Award recipients — honoring Distinguished Postgraduate Achievement for her impact in health, food access, and community service.
View announcement
🏆 Award
2025 Global Women Award
George Washington University & Global Woman P.E.A.C.E. Foundation
Dr. Roach was honored with the Global Women Award — recognizing her leadership in health equity, food access, and community transformation in Ward 8 and beyond.
View award
▶ YouTube
Interview with Dr. Roach — Howard University TV
Howard University Television (WHUT) · YouTube
Dr. Roach sits down with Howard University TV to discuss her mission, the food pantry, and her vision for eradicating health disparities in Ward 8 through clinical food access.

Join the Movement

We can't do this alone.

Churches, businesses, healthcare professionals, philanthropists — every partner multiplies the impact for Ward 8 families.

Get Involved

Three ways to partner
with The JJ Center

Your organization can make a direct, measurable impact on health and lives in Ward 8.

01

Underwrite Clinical Workshops

Fund educational materials, glucose & blood pressure monitoring supplies, and fresh food demo kits for our medical team's multi-week workshop series.

02

Sponsor Our DMV Grocery Route

Fund the weekly transportation costs of sourcing fresh, healthy food from grocery partners across DC, Maryland, and Virginia.

03

Join Our Medical & Volunteer Alliance

Healthcare professionals join our clinical volunteer rotation. General volunteers assist with dignified, community-facing food distribution events.

We welcome all partners: Churches · Mosques · Faith-Based Organizations · Civic Groups · Local Businesses · Healthcare Professionals · Philanthropists · Educators
✉ Contact Us 🤝 Volunteer Openings
